description: Decompose the balance of payments into current, capital, and financial accounts and analyse how capital flows interact with savings, investment, and the exchange rate

fields.name: current_account_pct_gdp
fields.label: Current account balance as percent of GDP

fields.name: capital_account_pct_gdp
fields.label: Capital account balance as percent of GDP

fields.name: financial_account_pct_gdp
fields.label: Financial account balance as percent of GDP

accounts.current_account: Trade in goods/services + primary income (wages, investment income) + secondary income (transfers)
accounts.capital_account: Capital transfers and non-produced non-financial assets (usually small)
accounts.financial_account: Direct investment, portfolio investment, other investment, reserves

identity.formula: CA + KA + FA = 0 (with reserves and errors & omissions)
identity.interpretation: Trade deficit is financed by net foreign asset purchases (FA surplus)

savings_investment.identity: CA = S - I (national savings minus investment)

twin_deficits.mechanism: Fiscal deficit (G > T) lowers national savings -> wider CA deficit

hot_money_risk.vulnerability: Rising external financing needs increase FX and rates risk

applications.fx_forecasting: Persistent CA deficits often weaken currency over medium term
applications.sovereign_analysis: Rising external debt + CA deficit -> sovereign risk premium rises
applications.equity_macro: Net exporters benefit from global demand; importers from domestic demand

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat current account deficits as inherently bad
anti_patterns.why: Emerging economies importing capital for productive investment can grow faster; context matters

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore the financing mix
anti_patterns.why: FDI-financed deficits are far more stable than those financed by short-term portfolio flows

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume twin deficits always hold
anti_patterns.why: Private savings changes can offset fiscal deficits (partial Ricardian effect)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use nominal CA figures across countries without scaling
anti_patterns.why: CA-to-GDP allows cross-country comparison; nominal levels mislead
